decisions.title: Why Function Calling Is the Key to Turning Chat into an Agent
decisions.description: The single most important transition from chatbot to agent is function calling. When the LLM can declare 'I want to call function X with arguments Y' and the host program executes it, the LLM gains the ability to affect the world. This is not just a feature — it is the architectural boundary between a chatbot and an agent.
decisions.alternatives: An alternative is to have the LLM emit structured text (like JSON or XML) that the host parses. While this works, it is fragile and requires custom parsing. Native function calling via the API is more reliable and standardized.

zh.title: 为什么函数调用是从聊天转变为 Agent 的关键
zh.description: 从聊天机器人到 Agent，最关键的一步就是函数调用。当 LLM 能声明「我要用参数 Y 调用函数 X」，而宿主程序负责执行时，LLM 就获得了影响外部世界的能力。这不仅仅是一个功能点，而是聊天机器人与 Agent 之间的架构分界线。

decisions.title: Why Start with a Single Tool
decisions.description: Lesson 1 introduces exactly one tool. This forces learners to focus on the mechanics of the tool-use protocol: how the LLM requests a call, how the host executes it, and how the result is fed back. With only one tool, there is no routing complexity — just the pure request-execute-respond cycle.
decisions.alternatives: We considered starting with two or three tools to make the demo more impressive, but this would shift attention from the protocol to the tools themselves. The protocol is what matters at this stage.

zh.title: 为什么从单个工具开始
zh.description: 第 1 课只引入一个工具。这迫使学习者专注于工具调用协议的机制：LLM 如何请求调用、宿主如何执行、结果如何回传。只有一个工具时，没有路由的复杂性，只有纯粹的「请求-执行-响应」循环。
